problem.summary: Define the ROCm custom-kernel boundary corresponding to CUDA TMA-style GEMM studies: explicit LDS tile staging, MFMA math, barrier/wait discipline, and comparison with CK/hipBLASLt.
outputs: C = A * B with FP32 accumulation
constraints: Template-only; no timing or profiler evidence is claimed
This is not NVIDIA TMA multicast; record the actual AMD LDS staging mechanism used
Keep gfx90a, gfx942, and gfx950 results separate
Record library baseline algorithm or CK collective whenever available

verification.method: Compare against CPU FP32 accumulation and a library GEMM baseline for identical inputs, dtype, layout, and math contract.

curation.notes: Second parity pass analog for CUDA task `tma-multicast-gemm`, translated into an AMD/ROCm LDS-staged MFMA competition boundary.
